title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) - Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with our easy recipe. This hearty, healthy, and flavorful Middle Eastern classic is perfect for any meal. Get the best Adas soup recipe here!

seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?
Step-by-Step: How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A warm bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a healthy and flavorful Middle Eastern classic.

description: Experience the comforting warmth of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, a truly soulful dish from the heart of the Middle East. This healthy and hearty vegan soup, rich with the flavors of lemon and fresh herbs, is a staple in Lebanese homes, perfect for a nourishing meal. It's incredibly easy to prepare yet delivers a depth of flavor that will transport your taste buds.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion, mince the garlic, and dice the carrot and potato. Have your vegetable broth and spices ready for cooking.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the diced carrot and potato, and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the ground cumin and turmeric, stir well to coat the vegetables, and cook for another minute until the spices are fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, then pour in the vegetable broth. Stir everything together, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer Until Tender
instructions.text: Allow the soup to simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender and the lentils have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the soup becomes too thick, you can add a little more hot broth or water.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. For a smoother consistency, you can use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup. I prefer it slightly chunky.

instructions.title: Serve Adas
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley, and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil if desired. Serve immediately with warm pita bread or a side salad.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, toast the cumin seeds briefly in a dry pan before grinding them, or use a pinch of seven-spice blend.
If you prefer a completely smooth soup, blend it with an immersion blender until creamy. For a rustic texture, blend only half of the soup and stir it back in.
Adas soup tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if needed.
article: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a beloved Middle Eastern Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
